entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A staple crop known for its abiotic stress tolerance and the subject of genome sequencing efforts.

entities.name: SUGARCANE
entities.description: A crop closely related to sorghum.

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A crop closely related to sorghum.

entities.name: ST. LOUIS, MISSOURI
entities.description: The location where the National Science Foundation-sponsored workshop was held.

entities.name: SORGHUM GENOMICS
entities.description: The field of study focusing on the genome of sorghum.

entities.name: GENOME SEQUENCING
entities.description: The process of determining the complete DNA sequence of the sorghum genome.

entities.name: GENETICALLY ANCHORED PHYSICAL MAP
entities.description: A map used to guide the assembly and annotation of the genome sequence.

entities.name: SEQUENCE ASSEMBLY
entities.description: The process of putting together the sequenced fragments of a genome.

entities.name: ANNOTATION
entities.description: The process of identifying functional elements within a genome sequence.

entities.name: COMPARATIVE GENOMICS
entities.description: The study of genomic structures across different species, specifically tropical grasses.

entities.name: TROPICAL GRASSES
entities.description: A group of plants that includes sorghum, sugarcane, and maize.

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: The inherent ability of sorghum to withstand non-biological environmental stresses.
